articles.title: SECURITY
articles.summary: Tomorrow kicks off CodeSecCon 2025 [4] — SecurityWeek's free two-day virtual event for developers who care about building secure, scalable apps.

articles.title: SQUASHING MY DUMB BUGS AND WHY I LOG BUILD IDS
articles.summary: A bug was introduced by a misplaced negation when handling user-agent headers in a C++ project. This mistake, caused by a copy-paste error while refactoring code to use "if scope" for error handling, resulted in user-agent data not being captured for a specific build. To clear up confusion for users and pinpoint the faulty data, the author added a visual "fingerprint" that represented the build's git commit hash to the reporting page, allowing users to quickly identify data affected by the buggy version.

articles.title: OPENFREEMAP SURVIVED 100,000 REQUESTS PER SECOND
articles.summary: The creator of OpenFreeMap was surprised to discover a massive surge in traffic, reaching 100,000 requests per second, due to a new collaborative drawing website called Wplace.live built on top of OpenFreeMap. This unexpected load caused some tiles to fail, but the service remarkably managed to handle most of the traffic. To help mitigate this traffic, they implemented a Cloudflare rule to limit Wplace.live's traffic and offered to help them set up their own self-hosted OpenFreeMap instance.

articles.title: ZIG'S LOVELY SYNTAX
articles.summary: The design choices behind the Zig programming language's syntax makes it clear and consistent to use. It has prefix types, raw strings, and a uniform approach where everything is treated as an expression. Integer literals without suffixes, the removal of the arrow in function definitions, and the use of keywords to denote control flow, including boolean operators, are other features that are shown in this article that make it a useful language.

articles.title: OPTIMIZING MY SLEEP AROUND CLAUDE USAGE LIMITS
articles.summary: This dev has been structuring their sleep schedule around the five-hour reset time of their Claude Pro subscription to maximize its usage for their B2B SaaS project. Inspired by the sleep patterns of solo sailors, they've adopted a polyphasic sleep schedule, napping in short intervals to coincide with Claude's token usage limits. This has greatly increased their productivity, allowing them to ship features quickly.

articles.title: AN ENGINEER'S PERSPECTIVE ON HIRING
articles.summary: Many companies waste time and fail to identify good candidates. Effective interviews are differentiated, applicable to the actual job, efficient with time, and make an assessment of the long-term value of the candidate. Live coding and take-home assignments are worse than work sample discussions and code reviews.

articles.title: THE IMPORTANCE OF OFFTOPIC
articles.summary: Having off-topic online spaces is important for building team camaraderie and improving productivity in remote work environments. A lack of informal online interaction negatively impacts team dynamics and work quality. Leadership must actively participate and cultivate a culture where off-topic communication is valued.

articles.title: IT JUST GOT EASIER TO CUSTOMIZE CLERK UI COMPONENTS
articles.summary: Clerk now supports theming via CSS variables and has a dedicated shadcn/ui theme that automatically matches your application's existing shadcn/ui theme configuration. Watch [12] as we walk you through how to style UI components.

articles.title: OPEN LOVABLE
articles.summary: Open Lovable allows users to instantly build React apps by chatting with AI. The project requires API keys from services like E2B and Firecrawl and, optionally, from AI providers like Anthropic, OpenAI, or Groq.

articles.title: BLUEFISH
articles.summary: Bluefish is a JavaScript diagramming framework that lets users build complex, reactive diagrams using UI-like components and relationship-based layouts.

articles.title: FLINTLOCK
articles.summary: Flintlock is a service for creating and managing microVM lifecycles. It supports Cloud Hypervisor and Firecracker VMMs, which helps create microVMs on bare-metal hosts for use cases like virtualized Kubernetes clusters and isolated workloads. Flintlock also has API-driven functionalities for creating, deleting, and managing microVMs, configuring metadata, using OCI images, and exposing metrics.

articles.title: FROM GPT-2 TO GPT-OSS: ANALYZING THE ARCHITECTURAL ADVANCES
articles.summary: This post compares OpenAI's new open-weight LLMs, gpt-oss-20b and gpt-oss-120b, with GPT-2 and Qwen3. There are architectural advances such as removing dropout, using RoPE for positional embeddings, incorporating Mixture-of-Experts, and more. The post also discusses the training process, reasoning abilities, optimization for single-GPU use, and benchmark performance of the gpt-oss models.

articles.title: THE CURRENT STATE OF LLM-DRIVEN DEVELOPMENT
articles.summary: LLM-powered coding tools have a trivial learning curve, but won't magically produce production-ready code. These tools are great with well-established standards, integration tests, and bug fixing, especially in strongly-typed languages like Rust, but struggle with complex frontend development and introduce unnecessary code complexity. Stability is a concern, with pricing changes and frequent updates making them unreliable.

articles.title: HOW I USE TAILSCALE
articles.summary: Tailscale is a WireGuard orchestration service that can connect devices and services. It has features like SSH support, MagicDNS, and the "funnel" and "serve" commands for exposing services. It's great for self-hosting enthusiasts.

articles.title: MINIFEED
articles.summary: Minifeed is a blog reader and search engine that curates human-written blogs, allowing users to discover, search, subscribe to, and save their favorite content.

articles.title: ONE MILLION SCREENSHOTS
articles.summary: A project displaying an interactive map of over one million website homepage screenshots.

articles.title: GPTS AND FEELING LEFT BEHIND
articles.summary: This dev is not able to replicate the widely reported successes others have had with AI coding tools like GPTs and finds them unproductive despite their occasional usefulness for smaller tasks.

articles.title: HOW I CODE WITH AI ON A BUDGET/FREE
articles.summary: You can code with AI on a budget by using the free tiers of multiple AI models in a browser setup.
